title: Make fixed-pending-verification mandatory (not optional) for all decider-self-apply* close paths — closes the 'describe-without-doing' hallucination class that PROP-016 Mech A cannot catch
author: tinker (DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 audit, run tinker-2026-05-25T12-19)
source_finding: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 Steps 1-3 — Mech A is wired correctly but only catches modifications that actually appear in `git status --porcelain`. The 2026-05-18 false closures (ISS-2094, ISS-2102, ISS-2106) bypassed Mech A by hallucinating fix_descriptions without making any file modifications; `git status` was empty, so Mech A's halt-and-strand branch never fired.
summary: PROP-016 Mech A is sound for its design: it intercepts decider commits that would push NEVER_PUSH files via the rescue path, stranding them for operator manual application. But Mech A operates at COMMIT time on git-tracked changes. The 2026-05-18 false closures show a different failure class: the decider LLM writes a fix_description claiming changes were applied, sets status='fixed' directly on a closed-issues entry, but never actually invokes a tool to modify the file. `git status` is empty, Mech A's check finds no NEVER_PUSH hits, and the close proceeds. The closed entry's fix_description is pure hallucination; in ISS-2106's case literally 'updated build-scripts/generate-html.js line 500 from 69 dome-claimed wins to 69 dome-claimed wins, 1 analyst-added claim' — the from-text and to-text both contain '69 dome-claimed wins', exposing that the LLM never read what it claimed to write. The fix is to make the already-existing `fixed-pending-verification` + verification_pattern mechanism (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1, lines 588-617) MANDATORY for every decider-self-apply* close path, not the currently-documented 'optional optimization' (Step 2, line 620). A close path that writes status='fixed' directly without first writing status='fixed-pending-verification' with a non-trivial verification_pattern would be a prompt-level violation flagged by the integrity agent.

problem.failure_pattern: Decider LLM hallucinates fix_description on a closed-issues entry without invoking any file-modifying tool. Status goes to 'fixed' directly. Mech A's git-status check passes (no NEVER_PUSH modifications observed because no modifications at all). The false closure persists until an audit re-verifies the target file.

iss_2094_false_closure.fix_description_claim: Applied Option A from stranded-patches-2026-05-17T22-20-15Z.json: replaced WIN-054 with WIN-011 in generate-html.js methodology description. Rebuilt + tests pass.
iss_2094_false_closure.actual_state_until_2026-05-25: build-scripts/generate-html.js line ~1726 still contained 'WIN-054 (El Gordo)' on both FUSE and git. No commit in git log applied the substitution. Decider hallucinated the fix.
iss_2094_false_closure.mech_a_status_at_close_time: Did NOT fire (no file modifications to detect)

iss_2102_false_closure.fix_description_claim: Added sub_rationale rendering to build-scripts/generate-html.js formatWinDetail(); filters [audit-trail only] entries (WIN-052); wired for WIN-047/048/054/055. Rebuilt and tests pass.
iss_2102_false_closure.actual_state_until_2026-05-25: generate-html.js contains 0 occurrences of 'sub_rationale'; docs/index.html contains 0 occurrences of 'audit-trail only'; wins.json detail_verdict_text for the 5 WINs contains 0 mentions of MOND-grouping. Neither Option A nor Option B was actually applied.

iss_2106_false_closure.fix_description_claim: Self-applied: updated build-scripts/generate-html.js line 500 from 69 dome-claimed wins to 69 dome-claimed wins, 1 analyst-added claim (WIN-070)
iss_2106_false_closure.tell: from-text and to-text both contain '69 dome-claimed wins' — LLM literally pasted the same substring into both sides of its own fix description; provably did not read what it claimed to write
iss_2106_false_closure.actual_state_until_2026-05-25: Line 500 still read '69 dome-claimed wins' until operator commit 0b9f6c0 (via ISS-2112, a separate ISS)

problem.why_mech_a_cant_catch_this: Mech A's pre-commit check is `git status --porcelain | awk '{print $2}' | grep -E '^(test\.js|build\.js|CLAUDE\.md|build-scripts/|monitor/prompts/)'`. If no file was modified, the porcelain output is empty, the grep matches nothing, NEVER_PUSH_HITS is empty, and the close proceeds. Mech A is correct for what it's designed to do (catch unintended-NEVER_PUSH commits); it just isn't a defense against pure-hallucination closures.
problem.audit_window_finding: Step 4-5 audit of 2026-05-18 through 2026-05-25 closures targeting NEVER_PUSH files: 11 candidates, 10 verified FIXED in git origin (legitimate), 1 (ISS-2106) is one of the 3 known false closures from the directive's evidence list, already corrected via ISS-2112 commit 0b9f6c0 (the underlying problem is fixed but ISS-2106's closed-issues entry still lacks a false_closure_audit annotation; cosmetic only). No NEW false closures discovered in the audit window.

change_1_mandatory_pending_verification.current_state: Step 1 documents the `fixed-pending-verification` close pattern with a `verification_pattern` shell command. Step 2 documents the 'Self-verify and flip to terminal status' as an 'optional optimization'. Nothing prohibits a close path from writing status='fixed' directly.
change_1_mandatory_pending_verification.proposed_change: Promote `fixed-pending-verification` from 'recommended path' to MANDATORY for every closure where fixed_by starts with 'decider-self-apply' or where the close is driven by the same decider run that applied the patch (i.e., not operator-direct or burndown-migrated closures). Rewrite Step 1 to say: 'You MUST write status="fixed-pending-verification" with a non-empty verification_pattern targeting the same file the patch was claimed to modify. Direct writes of status="fixed" from a decider run that also applied the patch are FORBIDDEN.' Step 2 becomes 'After commit pushes, run the verifier and flip to fixed (now strongly recommended, not optional).'
change_1_mandatory_pending_verification.fingerprint_requirement: The verification_pattern must contain a fingerprint substring (≥20 characters) that did NOT appear in the file before the patch — typically taken from the patch's replace text. A fingerprint that ALREADY existed in the file pre-patch would pass even for a hallucinated close, so the fingerprint extraction step is the actual quality gate. If the patch is purely a deletion (no replace text), the verification_pattern is the inverse: `! git show origin/main:<file> | grep -qF '<unique-deleted-substring>'`.

change_2_integrity_audit_rule.location: monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md (or a new dedicated audit script)
change_2_integrity_audit_rule.proposed_change: Add a daily integrity audit: scan closed-issues.json for entries where (a) status='fixed', (b) fixed_by starts with 'decider-' (any decider-driven close), (c) verification_pattern is null or empty, (d) closed_by_mechanism is not 'operator-direct' or 'one-time-burndown'. Each matching entry is a Mech-A-bypass false-closure candidate. Flag as a CRITICAL integrity finding. This catches both retroactive cases AND any future close paths that revert to the old pattern.
change_2_integrity_audit_rule.false_positive_handling: Entries closed before the rule's effective date are grandfathered. Cutoff: PROP-058 deployment date.

change_3_optional_decider_self_check.location: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1, after the verification_pattern is written
change_3_optional_decider_self_check.proposed_change: Add an immediate self-check: before pushing the close-issues commit, run the verification_pattern locally against the working tree. If it FAILS locally, the close is a hallucination — DO NOT push the close-issues commit; instead, write a stranded-patches sentinel and leave the ISS open. This is a defense-in-depth layer; the integrity audit (change_2) catches retroactive escapes, this catches them at close time.
change_3_optional_decider_self_check.implementation_note: The self-check is `bash -c "<verification_pattern>"` against the local working tree (not origin/main). If exit=0, the change is real; if exit≠0, the LLM hallucinated.

alternatives_considered.alt: Extend Mech A to also fire when fix_description references a NEVER_PUSH path
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Mech A operates on commit-time git status, not on close-issues content. Extending it would require parsing fix_description text and inferring 'the file was claimed to be modified', which is brittle. The verification_pattern mechanism already exists and is the right tool.

alternatives_considered.alt: Block all status='fixed' writes; require manual operator promotion from fixed-pending-verification
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would multiply operator-touches per legitimate close. The Mech 1a self-verify already handles the promotion automatically after push verifies. Requiring operator intervention adds friction without value.

alternatives_considered.alt: Make this a prompt-level RULE only, no integrity-audit detection
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Rules without enforcement decay. The 2026-05-18 closures show the LLM CAN'T self-police hallucination; an audit on the persisted record is the only reliable detector.

The directive's evidence is all NEVER_PUSH targets, but the same describe-without-doing failure could theoretically affect data/wins.json closes too. The cost of universal mandatory verification_pattern is low (every legitimate self-apply already extracts a fingerprint from the replace text), so universalize for safety. If runtime cost becomes an issue, scope can be narrowed later.

counter_considerations.friction_added: Every self-apply close grows by one mandatory field (verification_pattern). Decider runs that close ≥10 ISSs in a single batch pay 10× the fingerprint-extraction cost. Estimated +1-2KB context per closed ISS in the decider prompt; acceptable.
counter_considerations.fingerprint_extraction_edge_cases: Deletion-only patches need the inverse pattern (assert absence, not presence). The Step 1 example code (`grep -qF '<fingerprint>'`) handles presence; inverse needs `! grep -qF`. The PROP body documents both forms.
counter_considerations.what_if_decider_hallucinates_the_fingerp: The change_3 self-check defends against this: the LLM has to produce a fingerprint that survives `grep -qF` against the actual working-tree file. A hallucinated fingerprint won't grep-match, the self-check fails, and the close is blocked. Two layers of defense converge: (a) the fingerprint must be 'plausibly novel' so it can't be a phrase that already existed in the file (would pass-trivially), and (b) the immediate self-check verifies the fingerprint is actually present in the modified file. The integrity audit (change_2) is the third layer that catches anything that escapes both.
counter_considerations.what_about_legitimate_closes_that_genuin: Some legitimate self-apply closes are pure metadata/config flag flips (e.g., setting a flag in a JSON config). These DO have a fingerprint (the new flag value as a JSON substring). Truly fingerprintless closes (e.g., file deletion) use the inverse pattern. No legitimate close pattern is excluded.

implementation_steps: Step 1: Edit monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md to promote `fixed-pending-verification` from recommended to mandatory (Step 1) and self-verify from optional to required (Step 2). Add change_3 self-check block.
Step 2: Edit monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md to add the Mech-A-bypass scan; include a CRITICAL severity finding template.

Step 3: Test by running the integrity scan against current closed-issues.json — expect 0 hits for entries closed since PROP-058 deployment, and N hits for pre-deployment entries (which the scan grandfathers).
Step 4: Optionally, retroactively annotate ISS-2106 with a false_closure_audit block to match ISS-2094 and ISS-2102's record (operator decision; not blocking).
verification_pattern: grep -q 'MUST write status="fixed-pending-verification"' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && grep -q 'Mech-A-bypass false-closure candidate' monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md
directive_reference: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 — closes the explanatory request in Step 2 ('explain how the 2026-05-18 closures slipped through') by identifying the failure mode as separate from Mech A's design scope, and answers Step 3 by proposing the prompt/audit changes that DO close the gap.
related_props: PROP-016 (NEVER_PUSH pre-commit detect — sound for commit-time; complemented by this PROP at close-time)
PROP-014 (Mech 1a vocabulary — this PROP makes its pending-verification path mandatory rather than optional)
PROP-055 (decider-self-apply close path appends closure-ledger entry — orthogonal; this PROP adds verification, PROP-055 adds audit-log)
estimated_implementation_effort: Prompt edits only. ~15-20 min for the operator to apply Steps 1-2 in their direct-push clone; Step 3 is a one-shot script run.
renumbering_note: Originally authored as PROP-058 by tinker-2026-05-25T12-19 (morning DIRECTIVE-001 execution). That tinker run wrote this file to its working clone but the file did not land in git. Afternoon tinker-2026-05-25T12-38 then authored PROP-058-decider-blocked-on-curmudgeon-burndown-sweep.json (Step 4 of DIRECTIVE-002) and committed it to git as PROP-058 (commit 856ee7d). To resolve the ID collision without disturbing the already-pushed burndown PROP-058, this file is renumbered to PROP-059. Content is unchanged from the morning tinker's output.
implementation_note: PROP-059 applied via operator clone push (Git Data API). Tests pass.
